title: 10 best herbal creams for blocked nose

remedy_properties: Eucalyptus oil: Eucalyptus oil has natural decongestant and expectorant properties, helping to relieve congestion and sinus pressure by thinning mucus and making it easier to expel.
Cajeput oil: Cajeput oil contains cineole, a compound that helps to break down and clear out mucus from the nasal passages, providing relief from stuffiness and congestion.
Menthol: Menthol has a cooling effect on the nasal passages, helping to reduce swelling and open up airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving pressure in the face.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus, also known as Tasmanian blue gum, creams helps with blocked nose because of its decongestant properties. The oil extracted from its leaves contains compounds like eucalyptol, which work to thin and clear mucus. This helps to relieve congestion and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages. The cream's cooling sensation also helps to open up airways, making it easier to breathe. As a result, it provides quick and effective relief from blocked nose symptoms. This makes it a popular remedy for colds and sinus issues.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene acts as a natural decongestant, helping to thin mucus and relieve congestion in the nasal passages.
β-Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, which can contribute to blocked nose.
α-Pinene: This terpene has bronchodilatory properties, helping to relax the airway muscles and improve airflow, which can help alleviate blocked nose symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high eucalyptol content, which is a natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory agent.
Barks: Utilized for their rich eucalyptol and other bioactive compounds, which help in relieving nasal congestion and inflammation.
Stems: Employed for their eucalyptol content, which aids in reducing nasal congestion and promoting a clear airway.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves from eucalyptus globulus trees in early morning to prevent moisture loss.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm oven at 40 degrees celsius for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Combine 200g of dried leaves with 200ml of sweet almond oil in a glass jar for infusion.
Steep the mixture in a warm water bath at 40 degrees celsius for 2 hours to facilitate oil extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and whip 100g of beeswax with the infused oil to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Eucalyptus oil: Eucalyptus oil has natural decongestant and expectorant properties that help to relieve congestion and sinus pressure by breaking down mucus and opening up airways.
Peppermint oil: Peppermint oil's cooling and relaxing effects can help to ease nasal congestion and sinus pressure, reducing inflammation and promoting easier breathing.

Melaleuca alternifolia (Tea Tree) oil: Tea Tree oil has antimicrobial properties that help to combat bacterial and fungal infections that may be contributing to a blocked nose, promoting healthy mucous membranes and sinuses.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, creams helps with blocked nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredient, melaleuca oil, has decongestant properties that help to reduce nasal congestion and promote sinus drainage. By soothing and calming the nasal passages, tea tree cream provides relief from stuffiness and congestion, making it easier to breathe and feel more comfortable. Regular use can also help to prevent recurring nasal congestion. Its antimicrobial properties also help to combat infections that may be causing the blockage.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): It helps to break down and clear mucus from the nasal passages, providing relief from congestion and blocked nose.
Linalool: It has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing and relief from blocked nose.
Methyl eugenol: It exhibits expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, providing relief from congestion and blocked nose.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their essential oil content, which has decongestant and antimicrobial properties to help relieve nasal congestion.
Seeds: Used for their essential oil content, which has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties to help reduce nasal swelling and fight off infections.
Buds: Used for their essential oil content, which has decongestant and expectorant properties to help relieve nasal congestion and promote the removal of mucus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of melaleuca alternifolia and dry them in a low temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Infuse 2 ounces of dried leaves in 4 ounces of carrier oil such as jojoba oil for 4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix 1 ounce of the infused oil with 4 ounces of beeswax and 2 ounces of shea butter in a double boiler.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer until it thickens to a creamy consistency.

remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Sage creams contain expectorants like thymol, which help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in sage, such as rosmarinic acid, reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal tissues, helping to unblock the airways.
Antiviral properties: Sage contains antiviral agents that help combat viral infections that can cause nasal congestion and blockage, such as the common cold.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, creams helps with blocked nose because of its decongestant properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as eucalyptus and camphor, work to open up airways and clear mucus. The anti-inflammatory properties in sage help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, the aroma of sage has a natural expectorant effect, helping to loosen and clear mucus, providing relief from congestion and sinus pressure. This natural remedy can be an effective alternative to over-the-counter medications.
remedy_constituents: Thujone: Thujone, a terpene found in Salvia officinalis, acts as a decongestant by reducing nasal congestion and promoting drainage, thus helping to alleviate blocked nose symptoms.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a phenolic compound, exhibits anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce nasal swelling and inflammation, making it easier to breathe through a blocked nose.

Salvianolic acid B: Salvianolic acid B, a phenolic compound, has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties, which can help alleviate nasal congestion and promote nasal airflow.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Salvia officinalis are the most commonly used part in creams for a blocked nose due to their high content of essential oils, particularly thujone, which has decongestant properties.
Roots: The roots of Salvia officinalis are also used in creams for a blocked nose due to their ability to help reduce inflammation and improve respiratory function.
Stems: The stems of Salvia officinalis are used in creams for a blocked nose due to their high content of antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to soothe and calm the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves in the morning to ensure optimal potency and flavor.
Dry the harvested leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 1 hour.
Infuse dried salvia officinalis leaves in a carrier oil like coconut oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil and combine it with beeswax and shea butter in a double boiler.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and forms a smooth, creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing for easier breathing.
Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial agents fight off bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause nasal congestion and sinusitis, promoting a healthy nasal environment.
Expectorant properties: Thyme's natural expectorants help loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm in the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to breathe and relieve congestion.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, creams helps with blocked nose because of its decongestant properties. The essential oils present in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have natural expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. These properties help to reduce congestion and promote nasal discharge, relieving symptoms of a blocked nose. Additionally, thyme's antimicrobial properties can help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the congestion, making it a natural and effective remedy for a blocked nose.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol acts as a natural expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, thereby alleviating congestion and blocked nose.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol exhibits antimicrobial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms in the nasal passages, which can contribute to congestion and blocked nose.
Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol has a decongestant effect by thinning mucus and making it easier to expel, providing relief from blocked nose and congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their decongestant and expectorant properties to help relieve nasal congestion.
Stems: Used for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties to help soothe and calm the nasal passages.
Flowers: Used for their decongestant and antiseptic properties to help clear mucus and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried thymus vulgaris leaves and 500ml of distilled water to make herbal infusion.
Steep the dried leaves in the distilled water for 20 minutes at 90 degrees celsius to prepare the infusion.
Strain the infusion and discard the solids to obtain 400ml of herbal liquid.
Mix 20g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil with the herbal liquid to create a uniform blend.
Heat the mixture at 40 degrees celsius for 10 minutes to achieve a smooth cream texture.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as camphor and borneol, help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion.

Decongestant property: The essential oils present in rosemary, particularly 1,8-cineole, have a natural decongestant effect, helping to shrink swollen blood vessels in the nose and sinuses and promoting mucus drainage.

Antibacterial property: Rosemary's antimicrobial compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help combat infections that can cause sinusitis and other respiratory issues, reducing the severity of symptoms like a blocked nose.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary, creams helps with blocked nose because of its decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in rosemary, such as camphor and borneol, help to reduce nasal congestion by thinning mucus and promoting drainage. Additionally, rosemary's expectorant properties aid in loosening and expelling mucus, providing relief from congestion and sinus pressure. This natural remedy can be a gentle and effective way to alleviate blocked noses and promote a clearer breathing.
remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: This phenolic diterpene has anti-inflammatory and mucolytic properties, which help to reduce nasal congestion and promote easier breathing.
Rosmarinic acid: As a polyphenolic compound, it exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to alleviate nasal congestion and inflammation associated with blocked nose.
Bornyl acetate: This terpene has bronchodilatory properties, which help to relax the airway muscles and improve breathing in individuals with blocked nose.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of essential oils, particularly camphor, which has decongestant properties.
Flowers: Utilized for their fragrance and ability to help reduce inflammation, thereby relieving nasal congestion.
Stems: Employed for their contribution to the overall aroma and potential decongestant effects of the essential oils they contain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the morning when the dew is still present on the plant.
Combine 200g of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves with 400ml of distilled water in a large saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce the heat and let it simmer for 20 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl and discard the solids.
Mix 50g of beeswax with 20g of coconut oil and 20ml of the infused rosmarinus officinalis liquid in a small saucepan over low heat.

remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol helps to thin out mucus and reduce congestion by irritating the mucous membranes, causing them to produce more mucus and clearing out the nasal passages.
Eucalyptus Oil: Eucalyptus oil has natural decongestant properties that help to relax bronchial muscles, reduce inflammation, and ease breathing, making it easier to breathe through a blocked nose.
Camphor: Camphor helps to increase circulation to the nasal passages, which can help to loosen and clear out excess mucus, reducing congestion and promoting drainage.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gaultheria procumbens, also known as wintergreen, creams helps with blocked nose because of its natural decongestant properties. The cream is typically infused with the oil extracted from the leaves of the Gaultheria procumbens plant, which contains methyl salicylate. This compound helps to thin mucus, reducing congestion and making it easier to breathe. The anti-inflammatory properties of the cream also help to soothe and calm the nasal passages, providing relief from sinus pressure and congestion. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective way to alleviate blocked noses.
remedy_constituents: Gaultherin: Gaultherin is a sesquiterpene lactone that helps to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, providing relief from blocked nose.

Tannins: Tannins, specifically epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG) and epicatechin gallate (ECG), are polyphenolic compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help to reduce nasal congestion and alleviate blocked nose symptoms.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, particularly salicylic acid and gallic acid, are present in Gaultheria procumbens and exhibit anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to thin mucus, reduce nasal congestion, and provide relief from blocked nose.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make creams for a blocked nose because of their high content of essential oils, particularly methyl salicylate, which provides decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties.

Roots: They are used to make creams for a blocked nose because they contain salicin, a compound that is converted into salicylic acid in the body, providing anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects that help relieve nasal congestion.

Buds: They are used to make creams for a blocked nose because they contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate congestion, although the exact compounds responsible are not well-documented.
remedy_recipe: Harvest gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems in late summer for optimal essential oil production.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 3 hours.
Infuse 2 cups of dried gaultheria procumbens in 4 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer to a clean container.
Mix 1/4 cup of the infused gaultheria procumbens oil with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil to create a smooth cream consistency.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint contains anti-inflammatory compounds like menthol, which help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier for mucus to drain and relieving congestion.
Expectorant: The menthol and other volatile oils in peppermint have a natural expectorant effect, loosening and thinning mucus, making it easier to expel and clear out of the nasal passages.

Decongestant: Peppermint's ability to cool and numb the nasal passages helps to reduce swelling and ease congestion by constricting blood vessels, which in turn reduces swelling and relieves sinus pressure.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with blocked nose because of its natural decongestant properties. The menthol content in peppermint creams works by thinning mucus, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion. As the menthol is absorbed through the skin, it cools the nasal passages and helps to reduce inflammation, providing quick relief from stuffiness and congestion. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate nasal blockages and promote easier breathing.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Helps to thin and clear mucus, making it easier to breathe, by acting as a natural expectorant.
Menthone: Exhibits vasodilatory properties, which help to increase blood flow to the nasal passages, reducing congestion and inflammation.
Cineole (Eucalyptol): Acts as a natural decongestant by reducing inflammation and opening up airways, making it easier to breathe through the nose.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in menthol, which helps to relieve nasal congestion and ease breathing.
Stems: Like the leaves, the stems also contain menthol and other compounds that aid in decongesting the nasal passages.
Roots: The roots of Mentha x piperita contain menthone and other essential oils, which contribute to the decongestant and expectorant properties of the creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of mentha x piperita leaves and flowers in the early morning for optimal oil content.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 35°c for 24 hours.
Infuse 15g of dried mentha x piperita in 100ml of carrier oil like sweet almond oil at 60°c for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids within 5 minutes.
Mix 10g of beeswax and 20g of candelilla wax with 50ml of the infused oil at 60°c for 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing.
Antihistamine-like properties: Lavender's natural antihistamine effects help alleviate allergic reactions and reduce histamine release, which can contribute to a blocked nose.
Natural decongestant properties: Lavender's gentle warming and soothing action helps open up the nasal passages, reducing mucus buildup and congestion.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with blocked nose because of its decongestant and antiseptic properties. The essential oil extracted from the plant, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, have been found to thin mucus secretions and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages. As a result, the nasal congestion caused by allergies, colds, or sinus infections is alleviated, allowing for easier breathing and improved respiratory function. This natural remedy provides a soothing and calming relief from blocked nose symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that acts as a natural decongestant, helping to reduce nasal congestion by thinning mucus and promoting its drainage.
Linalyl acetate: A terpene that exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can contribute to blocked nose.
Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate nasal congestion by reducing inflammation and promoting the clearance of mucus.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain calming and decongestant properties that help to relieve nasal congestion.
Leaves: They possess antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and clear the nasal passages.
Stems: They contain antioxidants that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the nasal area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers on a sunny morning when the dew has evaporated.
Dry the flowers in a warm place for 2 weeks to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Steep 50g of dried flowers in 500ml of carrier oil for 2 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain 400ml of infused oil.
Mix 100g of the infused oil with 50g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemongrass cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as citral and geraniol, help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.
Decongestant properties: The camphor and menthol present in lemongrass cream help constrict blood vessels, reducing swelling and congestion in the nose, allowing for easier airflow.
Antibacterial properties: Lemongrass's antimicrobial compounds, including limonene and pinene, help fight off bacterial infections that can cause nasal congestion and blockages.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus, also known as lemongrass, creams helps with blocked nose because of its natural decongestant properties. The cream contains citral, a compound that helps to thin mucus and reduce congestion. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the nasal passages and sinuses, reducing swelling and promoting drainage. Additionally, the aroma of lemongrass oil in the cream can help to relax the nasal muscles and promote easy breathing, providing relief from blocked nose and sinus congestion. Regular use can lead to improved respiratory function.
remedy_constituents: Citral: Helps with a blocked nose by acting as a natural decongestant, reducing nasal congestion and inflammation.
Limonene: Contributes to the decongestant properties of Cymbopogon citratus creams by helping to break down mucus and promote nasal drainage.
Geraniol: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties that aid in reducing nasal inflammation and congestion, thereby relieving blocked nose symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in citral, a natural decongestant that helps to ease nasal congestion.
Rhyzomes: Contain oils with anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and ease breathing.
Leaves: Also contain limonene, a compound that helps to break down mucus and clear nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kg of fresh leaves from mature cymbopogon citratus plants in the early morning.
Dry the leaves in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Steep 100g of dried leaves in 1l of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the liquid from solids.
Blend 500g of the infused oil with 100g of shea butter and 50g of beeswax to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Eucalyptol: A natural decongestant that helps to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing.
Menthol: A natural analgesic and counterirritant that can help to relieve sinus pressure and congestion by numbing the nasal passages and reducing inflammation.
Curcuminoids: Anti-inflammatory compounds found in turmeric (the curry plant) that can help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Helichrysum italicum, also known as curry plant, creams helps with blocked nose because it contains compounds that reduce inflammation and congestion. The anti-inflammatory properties of the herb help to ease nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, the creams' decongestant properties help to clear mucus and debris from the nasal passages, providing temporary relief from blocked noses. This makes Helichrysum italicum creams a popular natural remedy for people suffering from sinus congestion and nasal blockages.
remedy_constituents: Neryl acetate: This terpene acts as a natural expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, thereby relieving congestion.

Phenylethanoid glycosides (such as italicoside): These phenolic compounds possess anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, which may contribute to a blocked nose.
Sesquiterpene lactones (such as italidione): These compounds have been shown to have mucolytic properties, helping break down and clear excess mucus, which can help alleviate nasal congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves of Helichrysum italicum are used to make creams for a blocked nose because they contain compounds like nerolidol and italidione that have decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties.

Flowers: Flowers of Helichrysum italicum are used to make creams for a blocked nose because they are rich in essential oils, including italidione, which has decongestant and anti-inflammatory effects.
Roots: Roots of Helichrysum italicum are used to make creams for a blocked nose because they contain compounds like sesquiterpenes that have anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest helichrysum italicum flowers in the morning when they are fully open to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the flowers at 50c in a dehydrator for 8 hours to prevent bacterial growth.
Infuse the dried flowers in a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at a 1:5 ratio for 4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the herbal extract.
Mix the herbal extract with a moisturizing cream base at a 10% concentration and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tree peony creams helps with blocked nose because of its natural decongestant properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as peppermint oil and eucalyptus oil, work to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, tree peony contains antioxidants that help reduce inflammation in the nasal tissue, further reducing congestion and discomfort. By using a tree peony cream, individuals can find relief from blocked noses and sinus pressure without relying on harsh chemical-based products.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Tree peony cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, help reduce swelling in nasal tissues and sinuses, relieving congestion and discomfort.
Expectorant properties: The expectorant compounds present in tree peony creams, like saponins, help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe through a blocked nose.
Antihistamine properties: Tree peony cream's antihistamine components, such as polyphenols, help reduce histamine production, which can contribute to nasal congestion, itching, and sneezing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Tree peony roots have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, alleviating symptoms of a blocked nose.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of the tree peony contains flavonoids, which have been shown to exhibit antihistaminic and bronchodilatory effects, helping to relieve nasal congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of fresh paeonia suffruticosa flowers in the morning and wash them gently with cold water.
Dry the harvested paeonia suffruticosa flowers using a low-temperature oven or sun drying method for 12 hours.
Crush the dried paeonia suffruticosa flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, then sift through a 60-mesh sieve to remove any lumps.
Combine 100g of the powdered paeonia suffruticosa with 20g of beeswax, 40g of coconut oil, and 20g of vitamin e oil in a double boiler, heating until the mixture melts.
Remove from heat and let cool to around 50°c before pouring into sterilized jars and storing in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with blocked nose because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages. The warming properties of ginger also help to loosen and clear out mucus, making it easier for the body to expel. Additionally, the antihistamine and decongestant properties of herbal extracts such as peppermint and eucalyptus in these creams further aid in relieving nasal congestion and promoting a clear breathing passage.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing.
Expectorant properties: The expectorant properties of ginger cream help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to expel and relieving sinus pressure.

Vasodilatory effects: Ginger's vasodilatory compounds, such as gingerol, help dilate blood vessels in the nose, which can increase blood flow and reduce inflammation, further alleviating congestion and stuffiness.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stem of the ginger plant contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties that help relieve nasal congestion.
Roots: Ginger roots are known for their warming and drying properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and sinus pressure associated with a blocked nose.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots of at least 500g and wash thoroughly under running water.
Dry the ginger roots in a low-temperature oven set at 60°c for 12 hours or until completely dry.
Grind the dried ginger roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered ginger with coconut oil, beeswax, and vitamin e oil in a ratio of 2:1:0.5 respectively, heating the mixture at 70°c for 15 minutes until it reaches a smooth consistency.

Pour the hot mixture into sterilized glass jars and let it cool to room temperature before capping and storing in an airtight container for up to 6 months or longer with proper preservation techniques.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with blocked nose because of its natural ability to thin mucus, reducing congestion and promoting easy breathing. The active compound, anethole, has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe irritated nasal passages and sinus cavities, allowing for easier airflow. Additionally, fennel's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out excess mucus, making it an effective remedy for relieving sinus pressure and congestion associated with a blocked nose.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Fennel's expectorant property helps to loosen and clear out mucus from the nasal passages, relieving congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help reduce nasal congestion and ease breathing.
Seeds: Contain oils with natural decongestant properties that help loosen mucus and open up airways.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20 grams of dried fennel seeds and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Combine the ground fennel powder with 100 milliliters of coconut oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60°c for 30 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it steep for an additional 24 hours to allow the herbal properties to infuse into the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Allow the infused oil to cool before whipping it with 10 milliliters of beeswax and 10 milliliters of shea butter until it forms a smooth and creamy texture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hyssop creams helps with blocked nose because of its natural decongestant properties. The herb hyssop has been used for centuries to alleviate respiratory issues, including congestion and sinus pressure. When applied topically as a cream, it helps to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy drainage. This relief from congestion allows for easier breathing and improved overall respiratory function, making hyssop creams a natural and effective remedy for blocked noses.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Hyssop cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing and relief from sinus pressure.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial properties of hyssop cream help combat bacterial and fungal infections that can contribute to a blocked nose, promoting healthy mucus production and clearance.

Expectorant properties: Hyssop's expectorant properties stimulate the body's natural ability to expel mucus and phlegm from the nasal passages, helping to relieve congestion and open up airways for easier breathing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing congestion and relieving sinus pressure.
Leaves: High in flavonoids, which help to relax the nasal passages and reduce swelling, providing relief from a blocked nose.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried hyssopus officinalis leaves and grind them into fine powder using mortar and pestle for about 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered hyssopus officinalis with coconut oil in a ratio of 3:7 and heat it gently over low flame for 15 minutes.
Add beeswax and shea butter to the mixture and stir until melted completely after 5 minutes at room temperature.
Allow the mixture to cool down and solidify at room temperature for about 30 minutes before whipping into a creamy consistency.
Transfer the herbal cream to sterilized jars and store it in refrigerator for up to 6 months while keeping it away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet marjoram creams helps with blocked nose because of its natural decongestant properties, which help to relieve nasal congestion and sinus pressure. The herb's essential oils, such as camphor and borneol, work together to open up airways, reduce inflammation, and promote drainage, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, sweet marjoram's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus, further alleviating blocked nose symptoms. By applying the cream topically, you can experience quick relief from a stuffy nose and sinus issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Marjoram's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as terpinen-4-ol and borneol, help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, thus relieving congestion and sinus pressure.

Expectorant property: The essential oils present in marjoram cream, like cineole and pinene, help loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to breathe.

Antimicrobial property: Marjoram's antimicrobial properties, which include compounds like thymol and carvacrol, help combat infections that can cause sinusitis and other respiratory issues, promoting a healthy nasal environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots of sweet marjoram are effective in reducing congestion and relieving sinus pressure.
Seeds: The tiny seeds of sweet marjoram contain expectorant properties that help loosen mucus and promote easy drainage, making them perfect for blocked nose relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of origanum majorana from your garden or store-bought organic herbs, measuring out 2 tablespoons for each batch.
Combine the harvested origanum majorana leaves with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a small saucepan and heat over low flame for 5 minutes.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain it through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bowl, discarding solids.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax pellets and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil to the infused coconut oil and stir until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into small tin containers and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before using as a natural remedy for blocked nose relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with blocked nose because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The cream contains flavonoids, which help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, pot marigold has natural antihistamine and decongestant properties that help to relieve congestion and sinus pressure. By applying the cream directly to the affected area, it helps to soothe and calm irritated tissues, providing instant relief from a blocked nose. This natural remedy is particularly effective for relieving symptoms of colds, allergies, and sinusitis.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold's active compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help combat infections and reduce swelling in the nasal passages, thus alleviating congestion.
Expectorant properties: The saponins present in pot marigold cream can help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel and relieving blocked nose symptoms.

Antioxidant and soothing properties: Pot marigold's antioxidant compounds can help protect the nasal mucosa from oxidative stress and damage caused by pollutants and irritants, while its soothing effects can calm irritated tissues and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages.
remedy_parts: Roots: Potent anti-inflammatory properties help reduce nasal congestion and swelling.
Seeds: Rich in antioxidants, seeds soothe irritated nasal passages and promote healthy mucus production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh or dried calendula officinalis flowers in the early morning for maximum potency.
Dry the calendula flowers in a warm oven at 60°c for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Combine the dried calendula flowers with 100 milliliters of coconut oil and infuse for 4 weeks, shaking daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and discard the solids; add 20 grams of beeswax and melt in a double boiler at 80°c for 10 minutes.
Mix the melted beeswax and calendula-infused oil with 50 milliliters of distilled water and whip until smooth, allowing to cool and set for 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with blocked nose because they contain natural ingredients that soothe and calm the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing. The herbal extract in valerian cream has a decongestant property that reduces swelling and congestion in the nose, making it an effective remedy for relieving stuffiness. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness and discomfort associated with blocked noses, providing fast and natural relief from nasal congestion.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian root's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe through a congested nose.
Vasodilatory effects: Valerian's ability to dilate blood vessels can help to open up airways and increase blood flow to the nose, reducing congestion and promoting drainage of mucus.

Sedative and decongestant properties: The sedative properties of valerian can help to calm irritated nasal tissues, while its decongestant properties can reduce excess mucus production and ease sinus pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which help to relieve respiratory congestion and open up airways, making them ideal for treating blocked noses.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, or underground stem, of the valerian plant is rich in valepotriates, compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties and help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, alleviating congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried valerian root extract containing 0.8-1% valepotriates from a trusted supplier and store it in an airtight container.
Combine 20 grams of beeswax pellets with 40 grams of sweet almond oil and 30 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add 10 grams of valerian root extract to the melted oil mixture and stir until well combined, allowing it to steep for at least 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container, pressing on the solids to extract as much liquid as possible, taking about 30 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken before pouring it into small jars or tubes, resulting in a cream with a shelf life of up to 12 months when stored properly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with blocked nose because they contain the natural anti-inflammatory properties of yarrow, which helps to reduce swelling in the nasal passages. The cream's soothing properties also ease congestion by loosening mucus and promoting easy drainage, allowing for clearer breathing. Additionally, yarrow's natural antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles in the nose, further relieving sinus pressure and discomfort caused by a blocked nose.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow cream reduces swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, helping to clear congestion and ease sinus pressure.
Decongestant properties: Yarrow contains compounds that help shrink blood vessels and reduce excess mucus production, which helps to alleviate stuffiness and promote easier breathing.

Antihistamine-like properties: Yarrow's flavonoids have been shown to inhibit the release of histamine, a chemical involved in allergic reactions, which can contribute to nasal congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in volatile oils and flavonoids, which help to reduce nasal congestion and inflammation.
Flowers: Yarrow flowers contain apigenin, a compound that relaxes muscles in the nasal passages, helping to alleviate sinus pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 30 grams of fresh achillea millefolium leaves in a warm oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried herbs into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle and store it in an airtight container for 24 hours.
Mix 10% w/w of the herbal powder with 90% w/w of a carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil at room temperature for 1 hour.
Transfer the mixture to a double boiler and heat it gently at 60°c for 30 minutes stirring occasionally until the mixture thickens.
Strain the cream through a cheesecloth into clean containers and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with blocked nose because of its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the nasal passages. The cream's active ingredients, such as apigenin and flavonoids, work to reduce swelling and ease congestion, making it easier for mucus to drain out and breathe freely. Additionally, chamomile has natural antihistamine properties that help alleviate allergic reactions, which can contribute to a blocked nose. As a result, using herbal chamomile creams can provide quick and effective relief from nasal congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing and relieving congestion.

Antihistamine-like properties: The flavonoids present in chamomile have a natural antihistamine effect, which helps to block histamine receptors that cause itching, sneezing, and other symptoms associated with allergies and sinus congestion.

Soften and moisturize mucus: Chamomile's emollient and expectorant properties help to soften and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel and clear out the nasal passages, providing relief from congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of apigenin, an antioxidant that helps to soothe and calm nasal passages.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers are rich in azulene compounds, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and ease congestion.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including 200g of beeswax, 400ml of coconut oil and 40g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers.
Melt the beeswax in a double boiler at 150°c for 10 minutes while monitoring its temperature.
Combine the melted beeswax with the coconut oil and stir until it reaches a uniform consistency after 5 minutes.
Steep the dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in boiling water for 30 minutes then strain and add the liquid extract to the oil mixture.
Pour the final cream into sterilized jars and let it cool at room temperature for 30 minutes before sealing and storing in a cool place.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for blocked nose are topical ointments containing plant extracts that help relieve nasal congestion by reducing inflammation and opening airways. These creams are particularly effective due to their decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The best herbal creams for blocked nose include Eucalyptus globulus, Rosmarinus officinalis, Echinacea purpurea, Melaleuca alternifolia, Thymus vulgaris, Zingiber officinale, Mentha x piperita, Lavandula angustifolia, Thymus serpyllum, and Salvia officinalis. If left untreated, a blocked nose can disrupt sleep, impact daily productivity, and even exacerbate underlying respiratory conditions, leading to a significant decrease in quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to blocked nose that are treated with herbal creams are eczema, acne, and skin irritation. These creams typically contain anti-inflammatory and antibacterial herbs like tea tree oil, chamomile, and calendula, which help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation. They can also be used to treat minor burns, cuts, and scrapes by promoting healing and preventing infection.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with blocked nose is eucalyptus and peppermint. Eucalyptus oil has natural decongestant properties that ease congestion, while peppermint oil helps to thin mucus and promote drainage. Adding a few drops of rosemary oil can also aid in reducing inflammation and opening up airways. When combined in a cream or balm, these herbs work synergistically to provide relief from blocked noses, making it easier to breathe and relax. Regular application can offer long-term benefits.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by PloS one, herbal creams may be beneficial for blocked nose due to their ability to improve nasal congestion and sneezing in patients with allergic rhinitis. The study, which included 29 randomised controlled trials with a total of 1879 participants, found that single medicinal plants may improve overall total nasal symptoms, including nasal congestion, compared to placebo (SMD -0.31, 95% CI -0.59 to -0.02; participants = 249; studies = 5). As a result, herbal creams may provide relief from blocked nose by addressing these specific symptoms, although further research is needed to confirm their efficacy.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with blocked nose because they contain eucalyptus oil, which is a natural decongestant and expectorant. The oil's active compound, eucalyptol, breaks down mucus and reduces inflammation in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing. When applied topically, the cream helps to loosen and clear out mucus, providing quick relief from congestion. Its cooling and refreshing properties also help to soothe the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and promoting a sense of comfort and relief.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): Acts as a natural expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe.
Alpha-pinene: Exhibits bronchodilatory properties, relaxing the muscles in the airways and increasing airflow to help alleviate congestion.
Beta-pinene: Possesses anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, reducing inflammation and calming the muscles in the airways to help ease congestion and promote easier breathing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Eucalyptus leaves contain eucalyptol, a compound that helps to open airways and relieve congestion.
Buds: Eucalyptus buds contain a mixture of oils, including eucalyptol, which helps to reduce inflammation and relieve blocked noses.
remedy_recipe: Measure 20 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves and add to a clean glass jar.
Combine 200 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil with 20 grams of beeswax in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes or until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Strain the infused eucalyptus globulus mixture into the saucepan with the beeswax mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and allow it to cool and set for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams helps with blocked nose because they contain the essential oil of rosemary, which has natural decongestant properties. The active compounds in rosemary oil, such as camphor and borneol, help to thin mucus and promote drainage, reducing nasal congestion. Additionally, rosemary oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the nasal passages, providing relief from blocked nose and sinus pressure. This natural remedy can be a effective alternative to over-the-counter decongestants.
remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: It helps by reducing inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.
Rosmarinic acid: It helps by acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, which helps to soothe and decongest the nasal passages.
Cineole (also known as eucalyptol): It helps by acting as a decongestant and expectorant, helping to thin mucus and clear the nasal passages.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and volatile oils, which help to ease congestion and promote nasal breathing.
Flowers: Contain rosmarinic acid, a natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory compound that can help to alleviate nasal congestion.
Stems: Also contain rosmarinic acid and other essential oils, which can help to reduce inflammation and ease breathing in the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 20g of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves in 100ml of carrier oil at room temperature for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove the solids.
Combine 50g of shea butter and 20g of beeswax in a double boiler to melt at 180-190°f.
Gradually add the infused oil and 10ml of distilled water to the melted shea butter mixture while stirring constantly.
Pour the cooled mixture into decorative containers and label them for storage and later use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with blocked nose because they contain anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that combat the underlying causes of congestion. The cream's active compounds, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid, work to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, thereby relieving pressure and promoting drainage. By addressing the root causes of congestion, Echinacea purpurea creams can provide fast and effective relief from blocked noses, making breathing and sinus health easier to manage. This natural approach can help alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Isoquinoline alkaloids: These compounds help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion associated with a blocked nose.
Cichoric acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, thereby relieving blocked nose symptoms.
Icariin: This flavonoid may help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow in the nasal passages, which can help clear mucus and relieve congestion associated with a blocked nose.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used for making creams for a blocked nose due to their anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and congestion.
Roots: Used for making creams for a blocked nose due to their immunomodulatory effects that help stimulate the immune system to fight off infections.
Leaves: Used for making creams for a blocked nose due to their antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial and viral infections causing the congestion.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 30g dried roots in 250ml hot water for 15 minutes to create echinacea extract.
Combine 50g beeswax with 20g coconut oil in a heat-proof bowl over a double boiler.
Add 10ml of the echinacea infusion to the beeswax mixture and stir until well combined.
Remove the bowl from the heat and stir in 5ml of vitamin e oil and 5 drops of lavender essential oil.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let cool at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with blocked nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. The essential oil extracted from the plant, known as tea tree oil, contains compounds that help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses. This allows for easier breathing and drainage of mucus, providing relief from congestion. Regular application of Melaleuca alternifolia cream to the nose and surrounding areas may also help to soothe and calm irritated tissues, promoting a clear and healthy respiratory system.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (eucalyptol): Acts as a natural expectorant, helping to break down and clear mucus from the nasal passages, relieving congestion.
Limonene: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, and also has a decongestant effect.
Pinene: Functions as a bronchodilator, widening airways and improving breathing, which helps to alleviate blocked nose symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high content of oil, particularly eucalyptol, which has decongestant properties.
Stems: Stems are also used to extract eucalyptol oil, which helps to relieve congestion and open airways.
Buds: Buds of Melaleuca alternifolia contain eucalyptol, which is used in creams to provide quick relief from blocked nose and sinus congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves and flowers at peak oil production time.
Steam distill the harvested leaves and flowers for 2 hours using a steam distillation apparatus.
Filter the distilled oil through a coffee filter to remove impurities and sediment for 30 minutes.
Mix 5 grams of the melaleuca alternifolia essential oil with 15 grams of a moisturizing base in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Whip the mixture for 5 minutes using a hand mixer to create a smooth and even cream texture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams helps with blocked nose because they contain the natural antiseptic and expectorant properties of thyme. The essential oils present in these creams, such as thymol and carvacrol, have decongestant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to reduce nasal congestion and mucus production. By promoting the clearance of mucus and reducing swelling in the nasal passages, Thymus vulgaris creams provide relief from stuffy noses and sinus congestion, making it easier to breathe and relax.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol helps with blocked nose by exhibiting expectorant properties, facilitating the removal of mucus and reducing congestion.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol contributes to relieving blocked nose by its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory actions, which help reduce nasal swelling and fight off underlying infections.
Bornyl acetate: Bornyl acetate aids in alleviating blocked nose by its bronchodilatory effects, allowing for easier breathing and increased airflow through the nasal passages.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are commonly used due to their antiseptic and decongestant properties, which help relieve nasal congestion.
Flowers: Thyme flowers are used for their fragrance and potential decongestant properties, which can help alleviate blocked nose symptoms.
Stems: Thyme stems are sometimes used due to their ability to retain the essential oils found in the leaves, which can be beneficial for relieving nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves on a dry morning to prevent moisture retention.
Dry the leaves in a single layer at 150f for 2 hours or until they crumble easily.
Mix 1/4 cup of dried leaves with 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a small saucepan.
Simmer the mixture over low heat at 150f for 1 hour to infuse the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and store it in a glass jar at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with blocked nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. The active compound, gingerol, present in Zingiber officinale has been shown to reduce swelling and ease congestion in the nasal passages. By applying the cream topically, the gingerol is absorbed into the skin, where it can help to break down mucus and promote drainage, providing quick and effective relief from blocked nose symptoms. This natural remedy can be a helpful addition to your cold and flu treatment.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds help to break down mucus and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing.
Shogaols: Shogaols have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to breathe.
Zingiberene: Zingiberene has bronchodilatory effects, which help to relax the muscles in the airways and improve airflow, providing relief from blocked nose.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used because they contain high concentrations of active compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help relieve congestion.
Roots: Roots are used because they contain a combination of oils and compounds that help to decongest and warm up the nasal passages.
Leaves: Leaves are used because they contain a higher concentration of essential oils than the roots, which provide a stronger decongestant effect.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes in late fall or early winter when they're fully mature.
Clean and peel the rhizomes then chop them into small pieces for efficient infusion.
Steep 1 part of chopped rhizomes in 2 parts of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at 40% strength.
Strain the infused oil mixture using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter after 2-4 weeks of steeping time.
Whip the strained oil mixture with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of distilled water to create a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams helps with blocked nose because they contain menthol, a natural decongestant that quickly relieves nasal congestion. Menthol's cooling and warming properties help to thin mucus and reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe. The anti-inflammatory properties of menthol also help to soothe and calm the nasal tissues, providing long-lasting relief from stuffiness and congestion. This natural remedy provides quick and effective relief from nasal blockages.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Helps to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel from the nasal passages.
Limonene: Exhibits expectorant properties, promoting the removal of excess mucus and debris from the airways.
Rosmarinic acid: Possesses anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses to alleviate congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which provide decongestant and expectorant properties.
Stems: The stems of Mentha x piperita are also used due to their ability to release oils that help to ease congestion and promote sinus drainage.
Roots: The roots of the plant are sometimes used in creams to provide a more sustained release of menthol and other active compounds, offering longer-lasting relief from nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Combine 100g of dried mentha x piperita leaves with 500ml of boiling distilled water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes then strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Mix 100g of beeswax with 100g of cocoa butter in a double boiler over low heat.
Combine the infused liquid from step 2 with the beeswax-cocoa butter mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into glass jars and store in the refrigerator to solidify the mentha x piperita cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with blocked nose because of its decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds, including linalool and linalyl acetate, work together to open up airways and reduce swelling in the nasal passages. This allows for easier breathing and relief from congestion. Additionally, the soothing and calming effects of lavender can also help to promote relaxation and reduce stress, which can exacerbate respiratory issues. As a result, using Lavandula angustifolia creams can provide quick and effective relief from blocked noses.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Acts as a natural decongestant by thinning mucus and promoting its expulsion from the nasal passages.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling in the nasal passages and alleviating congestion.
Caryophyllene oxide: Possesses expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages and sinuses.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in relieving congestion and soothing the nasal passages.
Flowers: Utilized for their calming and decongestant properties, which aid in reducing inflammation and opening up airways.
Stems: Employed for their expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 10 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and set aside for later use.
Combine 100 milliliters of distilled water and 50 milliliters of witch hazel in a saucepan.
Combine the dried flowers and 250 milliliters of distilled water in a separate saucepan and heat gently.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard solids immediately after use.
Add 50 grams of shea butter and 20 grams of beeswax to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus serpyllum creams helps with blocked nose because of their decongestant properties. The key ingredient, thyme, contains essential oils that possess natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These oils help to break down mucus, reduce nasal congestion, and alleviate sinus pressure. Thymus serpyllum creams also have antiseptic properties that help to combat infections and promote healing in the nasal passages. As a result, they provide quick relief from blocked noses and sinusitis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Acts as a natural expectorant, helping to thin mucus and relieve congestion, making it easier to breathe through a blocked nose.
Carvacrol: Exhibits antimicrobial properties, reducing the growth of pathogens that can exacerbate nasal congestion and inflammation, thus helping to alleviate blocked nose symptoms.
Piperitenone: Possesses expectorant and decongestant properties, which can help to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, providing relief from blocked nose.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make creams for a blocked nose because of their high thymol content, which has decongestant and expectorant properties.
Flowers: Used to make creams for a blocked nose because of their fragrance and antiseptic properties, which help to clear the nasal passages.
Stems: Used to make creams for a blocked nose because of their thymol content, which helps to reduce inflammation and promote drainage in the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 20g of dried thymus serpyllum in 200ml of olive oil at a temperature of 70°c for 2 hours.
Strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth to separate the solid and liquid components.
Combine the infused oil with 10g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes to achieve a smooth and even consistency.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify completely before transferring it to a suitable container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis creams helps with blocked nose because they contain anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that ease congestion. The active compounds in Salvia officinalis, such as rosmarinic acid and borneol, work together to reduce nasal swelling and promote the thinning of mucus, making it easier to expel. Additionally, the decongestant properties of Salvia officinalis help to clear airways, relieving sinus pressure and congestion, providing quick relief from blocked noses. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective solution for nasal congestion.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and ease congestion.
Carnosic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant properties, which help protect the nasal mucosa from oxidative damage and promote healthy nasal function.
α-pinene: This terpene has a decongestant effect, which helps to relax the muscles in the nasal passages and increase airflow, relieving blocked nose symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used to make creams for a blocked nose because of their high content of essential oils, particularly thujone, which has decongestant and expectorant properties.
Roots: The roots are used to make creams for a blocked nose because they contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages.

Stems: The stems are used to make creams for a blocked nose because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help to relieve nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 50 grams of dried salvia officinalis leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground powder with 50 grams of beeswax and 30 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes while occasionally stirring until the wax and butter melt completely.
Remove the double boiler from the heat source and let it cool for 10 minutes then stir in 20 grams of vitamin e oil.
Pour the cooled mixture into small containers and allow it to solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes before use.
